# txKit

> Open transaction-safety primitive for Web3. Decode, preview, and simulate any
> transaction before it's signed. An open standard built for the agent era,
> useful today for every dApp, multisig, and DAO.

txKit is open-core: the SDK and protocol spec are Apache 2.0; verification is
offered as a hosted SaaS at verify.txkit.dev. txKit never holds keys - it sits
on the pre-signing layer between transaction producers (AI agents, dApp UI,
multisig coordinators) and signers (wallets, OWS endpoints, hardware devices).

## Core packages

- `@txkit/tx-protocol` - PreparedEnvelope discriminated union spec, CAIP-2,
  EIP-5792 batches, EIP-712 signatures, ERC-7730 clear signing, ERC-7715 wallet
  capabilities. 3 implemented kinds (evm-tx, evm-batch, signature) + 9 reserved.
- `@txkit/core` - framework-agnostic types and utilities.
- `@txkit/react` - React components (TxKitProvider, ConnectWallet, TokenBalance,
  TransactionButton, FlowSteps).
- `@txkit/themes` - CSS variable tokens and visual variants.
- `@txkit/tx-decoder` - calldata decoder registry.
- `@txkit/ows-adapter` - integration with OWS sign endpoints.
- `@txkit/x402-adapter` - integration with Coinbase x402 agent payments.

## Documentation

- Spec: https://docs.txkit.dev/protocol/prepared-tx
- Components: https://docs.txkit.dev/components
- Examples: https://docs.txkit.dev/examples
- GitHub: https://github.com/txkit/mono

## Notebook

- Blog index: https://txkit.dev/blog/
- RSS feed: https://txkit.dev/blog/rss.xml
- Long-form notes on Web3 transaction safety, AI agents, stablecoin treasury UX,
  and the design reasoning behind the txKit components and PreparedTransaction
  Envelope spec. Posts 1-2 times per month.

## Positioning

- Useful today for: dApp developers, DAOs and multisigs, DeFi frontends, wallets
- Built for the agent era: AI agents proposing on-chain actions, per-agent
  allowance grants, stablecoin payment flows (x402, Skyfire), ERC-7715 scoped
  capabilities

## License

Apache 2.0 on all published packages.
